Title: Arabidopsis root lipid droplets are hubs for membrane homeostasis under heat stress, and triterpenoid synthesis and storage

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LD-associated acyltransferase in roots, supporting lipid droplet–ER connectivity during membrane lipid remodeling under heat stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; transient expression subcellular localization in Nicotiana tabacum pollen tubes and Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis root LD-enriched fraction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 24 h heat stress at 37°C; high-sucrose axenic root culture for LD isolation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT9 was strongly enriched in Arabidopsis root LD fractions. 2. Transient expression showed LD localization in pollen tubes and partial LD localization in leaves. 3. The study implicates GPAT9 as a root LD-associated enzyme linked to lipid remodeling rather than as a direct determinant of a single nutrient metabolite.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic activity is in glycerolipid metabolism, the paper's main causal focus is LD association and organelle function, not direct metabolite engineering of GPAT9.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GPAT5 is a root LD- and ER-localized acyltransferase candidate, consistent with a role in LD-associated lipid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; transient localization in tobacco pollen tubes and N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis root LD-enriched fraction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress context for root LD proliferation; high-sucrose axenic root culture for proteomics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT5 was highly enriched in root LD fractions. 2. It localized to LDs and ER in pollen tubes and to LDs in leaves. 3. It supports the concept that root LDs host active lipid metabolic enzymes.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LPEAT1 localizes to root LDs and ER, indicating LD involvement in phospholipid remodeling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; transient localization in pollen tubes and N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis root LD-enriched fraction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress context for lipid remodeling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPEAT1 was enriched in root LD fractions. 2. It localized to LDs and ER in pollen tubes and clearly to LDs in leaves. 3. These data support LD participation in membrane phospholipid homeostasis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LEW1 was LD-enriched and localized around LDs in leaves, suggesting that root LDs recruit additional isoprenoid-metabolism enzymes beyond oxidosqualene cyclases.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; transient localization in pollen tubes and N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum in pollen tubes; lipid droplet-associated in leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis root LD-enriched fraction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-sucrose axenic root culture; heat stress context in the study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LEW1 was identified among LD-enriched root proteins. 2. It localized to ER in pollen tubes but around LDs in leaves. 3. The study links root LDs with broader isoprenoid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a central causal determinant of the measured triterpene accumulation compared with THAS1/MRN1.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Previously known LD-localized cycloartenol synthase is supported here as part of the root LD-associated triterpene/sterol metabolic hub.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; metabolite enrichment analysis discussed with prior localization evidence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in all five root LD replicates
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-sucrose axenic root culture; heat stress examined globally in study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAS1 was strongly enriched in root LD fractions. 2. The study interprets cycloartenol and cycloartenyl esters as LD-enriched metabolites consistent with CAS1 localization. 3. CAS1 supports the conclusion that LDs are hubs for triterpene synthesis and storage.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core to LD-associated triterpene metabolism concept, but not newly functionally localized here by transient assays.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Thalianol branch of oxidosqualene-derived specialized triterpene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to form the triterpene thalianol.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining oxidosqualene cyclase step; branch-switch enzyme for thalianol formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root specialized metabolite implicated in shaping the Arabidopsis root microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: THAS1 localizes to root LDs, and its substrate and products are enriched in LD fractions, supporting LDs as hubs for thalianol synthesis and storage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; transient localization in Nicotiana tabacum pollen tubes and Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; GC-MS metabolite profiling of isolated LD fractions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots; reported as root-specific/high in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-sucrose axenic root culture for LD isolation; heat stress at 37°C for global root LD remodeling analyses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS1 was highly enriched in Arabidopsis root LD fractions. 2. Transient expression confirmed LD localization in both pollen tubes and N. benthamiana leaves. 3. The substrate 2,3-oxidosqualene precursor squalene and the product thalianol/thalianol esters were enriched in LD fractions, supporting local synthesis/storage on LDs.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics: PRIDE PXD051152; PXD068568; metabolomics/lipidomics datasets S5, S11, S17-S20
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream thalianol-pathway enzymes THAD and THAH localized to ER/reticular structures rather than LDs, suggesting compartmental separation after cyclization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering root specialized triterpene content and microbiome-modulating metabolites by manipulating LD-associated triterpene branch flux.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Marneral branch of oxidosqualene-derived specialized triterpene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to form the triterpene marneral.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized triterpene metabolite branch in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MRN1 is an LD-localized oxidosqualene cyclase, indicating that root LDs support marneral-branch triterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LD proteomics; transient localization in pollen tubes and N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reported as strongly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-sucrose axenic root culture; heat stress context in study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MRN1 was enriched in Arabidopsis root LD fractions. 2. Transient expression showed clear LD localization in both heterologous systems. 3. Together with THAS1 and CAS1, MRN1 supports the conclusion that root LDs are hubs for triterpene synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct marneral metabolite enrichment in isolated LD fractions was not detailed to the same extent as thalianol; inference is based on enzyme localization and pathway logic.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential branch-control target for engineering specialized root triterpenes via LD-associated synthesis.
